eras.role: Generative AI Committee / Playwright Testing SME / Observability Guild
eras.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
eras.description: Rebuilding legacy backend systems, writing automated test suites, and building secure internal AI tools.
long_description: Core System Modernization: Worked as a key engineer replacing an old, vendor-built Java system with a decoupled case management platform running on a Flowable state engine. Moved complex, aging PowerBuilder logic into standalone Drools rule instances.
AI Tooling & Automation: Built a custom internal Copilot assistant that handles technical documentation layout across the department, dropping manual engineering README work by 60% to 70%.
Automated Test Engineering: Designed and shipped a comprehensive integration test suite using Playwright within a single sprint, stopping two major production bugs from making it to release.

Team Mentorship & Training: Ran cross-functional technical training and knowledge-sharing sessions, mentoring cohorts of 20 to 30 developers to get new hires up to speed and improve team coding standards.
System Observability: Served as a subject matter expert for monitoring frameworks, setting up critical metric tracking to give teams clear visibility into system uptime and performance bottlenecks.

eras.title: Senior Software Engineer
eras.description: Building localized dev environments, designing migration layers, and fixing enterprise e-commerce platforms.
long_description: Platform Migration Layer: Designed and wrote a custom facade-based bridge architecture for a major e-commerce search migration. Kept full backward compatibility with the old system intact, cutting the estimated timeline by ~50% and allowing a safe, phased rollout.

DevOps Environment Automation: Owned the move to containerize our internal tools. Wrote Docker environment-as-code setup scripts that completely stopped configuration drift and cut new developer setup times from 2.5 days down to ~4 hours.
E-commerce Code Cleanup: Cleaned up legacy code paths and resolved 10 to 20 critical production defects across the primary global e-commerce engine using C#, Angular, and Sitecore.

Pipeline Quality Control: Standardized automated testing in environments without Test-Driven Development by containerizing our Selenium grid builds, saving ~2 hours of pipeline run time and stopping flaky build failures.

eras.title: Software Engineer III
eras.role: Acting Technical Lead / Product Support Break-Fix Lead
eras.description: Managing high-volume transactional engine stability, cross-shore team triage, and emerging AI prototyping.
long_description: High-Volume Engine Scalability: Oversaw the architectural stability of core backend packaging, booking, and availability engines processing 3,000 to 5,000 daily transactions and 1.5M to 3M availability lookups per day.

Production Escalation & Triage: Led the dedicated 'Break-Fix' engineering team for three years, directing 2 internal and 4 offshore engineers. Resolved 8 to 20 critical production issues weekly and designed diagnostic tools that drastically reduced time-to-resolution.

System Performance Tuning: Successfully reduced a critical 5-minute availability engine timeout down to ~2 minutes, preventing thread pool exhaustion and vastly stabilizing system performance during peak traffic windows.
Emerging Tech & R&D: Collaborated directly with Microsoft's enterprise architecture team to prototype a multi-channel natural language booking engine using SMS, Messenger, LUIS, and internal APIs.

Technical Enablement & Community: Authored comprehensive architectural sequence flows and data models to standardize onboarding. Represented the firm at COSBE 'Be the Spark' events, presenting custom live-translation AI demos to cohorts of 30 to 40 students.

eras.title: Software Engineer & Consultant
eras.description: Bridging technical gaps and keeping core engines stable during a corporate joint venture.
long_description: Offshore Technical Lead: Served as the main technical point of contact between the US business and the offshore engineering team within Breakfix support. Used my knowledge of the core platform to guide remote developers, review code, and fix complex bugs across teams.
Engine Maintenance: Handled full-stack development and daily maintenance for the high-traffic Dynamic Packaging Engine, ensuring booking paths stayed online and functional.
Legacy System Troubleshooting: Dug into undocumented legacy code to find and fix deep architectural issues, keeping remote teams unblocked and moving forward.
Consultancy Delivery: Delivered production-ready code under strict consulting contracts, making sure technical features were completed on time and within the partner company's budget boundaries.

eras.title: Software Developer I - II
eras.role: Full-Stack Dev / API Implementation / Subject Expert for Data Entry System
eras.description: Building travel booking logic, running framework upgrades, and fixing codebase security vulnerabilities.
long_description: Core Engine Development: Wrote full-stack code and business rules for the core Dynamic Packaging and Booking engines to bundle and process high-volume vacation packages.
Framework Upgrades: Ran the technical execution for the company-wide migration from .NET 2.0 to .NET 4.0, which included rebuilding legacy codebases to run smoothly on Visual Studio 2010.

Security & PCI Compliance: Refactored aging application code to patch security vulnerabilities under the OWASP Top 10 framework, ensuring the core booking platform passed strict PCI compliance audits for credit card processing.
Service Architecture: Built and maintained WCF services to handle data exchange across different internal systems, and wrote dedicated Windows Services to process heavy background data tasks.
Distributed Teams: Documented system components and collaborated with remote offshore teams to keep development tasks and bug fixes moving consistently across the codebases.

eras.company: ITT Technical Institute & University of Wisconsin System
eras.title: Adjunct Instructor / Teaching Assistant
eras.description: Teaching core computer science foundations, programming logic, and hardware systems architecture.
long_description: Programming Logic Instruction: Taught core courses covering programming logic, syntax, data structures, and coding conventions. Managed classrooms of 20 to 30 students, balancing theoretical lectures with hands-on development labs.

Systems & Hardware Architecture: Led lectures and practical lab sessions on computer architecture, hardware components, and systems analysis. Designed interactive lessons on hardware troubleshooting, diagnostics, and board-level repair methods.

Web Development Support: Served as a Teaching Assistant for Web Application Development at UW-Milwaukee, providing direct technical support, code reviews, and debugging assistance to students building web software.

Curriculum & Delivery Management: Coordinated directly with Department Deans to manage curriculum delivery, grading rubrics, and final examination standards, ensuring all classroom metrics met institutional requirements.

Html_Text_Editor.title: Html Text Editor
Html_Text_Editor.description: A consolidated, browser-based toolkit providing a unified interface for common text transformations, code formatting, and data manipulation.
Html_Text_Editor.long_description: I developed an advanced text editor for custom work. It started as console and winform applications, then evolved into a single web-based app, and now it's a PWA. It can load files from your desktop and save them in the browser cache or as a download.

WeatherWallpaper.title: WeatherWallpaper
WeatherWallpaper.description: Dynamic wallpaper daemon that responds to weather conditions and time of day, with multi-source weather resilience and extensible plugin architecture.
WeatherWallpaper.long_description: I developed a weather app using Yahoo as the source for data, but lost the source code. I rewrote the app to include multiple data sources in case of outages, and added the ability to display different wallpapers based on time of day.

Tic_Tac_Math.title: Tic Tac Math
Tic_Tac_Math.description: A simple math tic tac toe game to teach children basic math.
Tic_Tac_Math.long_description: Back in school around 2003 or 04, I developed a Tic Tac Toe game using Visual Basic 6 and Microsoft Agents for input and output. Recently, I found the original source code and rewrote it in .NET, consolidating the logic into a single program with options to switch math types (divide, multiply, add, and subtract). As a side project during some time off, I converted the game to JavaScript and HTML, and brought back the Agents and text-to-speech functionality through the browser.
tech: Installers (nullsoft and Microsoft One Click Installer)

RiverScriptBot.title: RiverScriptBot
RiverScriptBot.description: A UI and NodeJS Rivescript server chatbot.
RiverScriptBot.long_description: I have created several chatbots over the years, including one in Vue.js and another in Angular, as well as a REST-based bot using Rivescript and Node.js. My interest in chatbots began in the early 2000s when I worked on an AIML project that used MS Agent, TTS, and speech recognition. Later, I worked with the Alice project and presented a version of my project at the MMAC Be The Spark tour, which utilized Microsoft cognitive services and a vocal chatbot capable of detecting and translating on the fly. I recently migrated the AIML code to RiveScript to create this chatbot.

Flag_Fairway.title: Flag Fairway
Flag_Fairway.description: A self hosted internal facing configuration mananger
Flag_Fairway.long_description: Trying a number of feature flag products, I found that there was a lot of bloat when it came to both performance, size, and message structure, so I decided to write up a simple REST CRUD app to be able to manage and configure feature flags within my own applications internally.

CloudyPie.title: CloudyPie
CloudyPie.description: A rewrite of DJAkbar's cloudy-a project
CloudyPie.long_description: Refactored and upgraded a Python-based lamp project for Raspberry Pi that displays weather information, originally created by DJAkbars, to improve operation timing and service calling, and migrate to Python 3.

Simple_Weather_Tray.title: Simple Weather Tray
Simple_Weather_Tray.description: lightweight weather tray app for Mac, Linux, and Windows
Simple_Weather_Tray.long_description: The app is a weather tool that runs on Mac, Linux, and PC and is based on weatherwallpaper. The code is compiled using GTK and allows the user to change the engine behind it. The idea for this app goes back to the developer's college days when they disliked the adware and spyware associated with Weathebug.

Desert_Changer.title: Desert Changer
Desert_Changer.description: Time based wallpaper changer for Windows
Desert_Changer.long_description: For Windows, This app changes your wallpaper throughout the day without the need for external calls, similar to Mac OS Mojave.

PodLoad.title: PodLoad
PodLoad.long_description: a .NET application designed for pulling video and audio podcasts via RSS feeds. It has been rewritten to support the .NET core framework and works on Linux as well.
tech: Basic file transfer over the network

Dif.title: Dif
Dif.long_description: This program helps you see what files have changed when you copy them from one folder to another. It goes off LastUpdated timestamps of the files

Clipboard++.title: Clipboard++
Clipboard++.description: Multi item clipboard manager for Windows
Clipboard++.long_description: Its purpose is to provide an application that can maintain an in-memory cache of multiple clipboard items. This is useful in case accidental copying over data occurs, as users can go back to the original version. Clipboard++ is a lightweight clipboard manager that offers various features such as encryption, in-memory compression, the ability to retain its state after reboots or restarts, and the option to save files directly from the clipboard.

WinFrost.title: WinFrost
WinFrost.description: a simple site-specific browser generator and browser
WinFrost.long_description: WinFrost is a web application that aims to provide users with the ability to access web pages as full-blown apps. The project was born out of a desire to see web pages become more like applications. Unfortunately, other projects like Mozilla Prism have been discontinued, leaving a void in the market. While Chrome has its own solution through shortcuts, that feature is also going away. WinFrost is inspired by Pepperment's Ice SSB app and is currently only available for Windows. Due to its dependence on Nuget updates, the project is not released as a standalone application, and developers are encouraged to compile the latest version themselves.

gpsdApi.title: Gpsd API
gpsdApi.description: A python based app to expose gpspipe as a rest API
gpsdApi.long_description: This is a Flask application that interacts with a GPS device using the gpspipe command-line tool to retrieve GPS data and check the device's connection status.

showcases.title: Enterprise AI Gateway
showcases.description: Designing secure architectural perimeters for local-first LLM deployment.

showcases.title: Legacy API Modernization
showcases.description: Re-engineering monolithic travel-tech infrastructure into scalable micro-utilities.

blog.title: The Pragmatic AI Champion
blog.excerpt: Vetting local LLMs without leaking company IP or compromising data sovereignty.

blog.title: Woodshop & Digital Logic
blog.excerpt: How the precision of physical joinery informs the architecture of scalable systems.

archive.title: UWM - Reusablity with IFrames
archive.description: My Softmore and early junior year personal site. Uses IFrames for reusability, and javascript dynamic content.

archive.title: UWM - Less noise
archive.description: removed footer, and replaced with a header. Origionally supported pulling my Blog off blogger. MacOs inspired theme.

archive.title: UWM - last iteration
archive.description: More tradtional attempt, with a template theme, but harder to maintain. Iframed limited to links page only.

archive.title: V1
archive.description: First iteration of the current site, built first with a template libary then with Vue 2.0 amd bootstrap. Content seperated in a JSON file and pulled

archive.title: V1.1
archive.description: Changed elements of UI, and removed large background panels.

archive.title: V2
archive.description: Migrated to Vue3. Full redisign of the site, colors, and layout, including an optional video background.

archive.title: V2.1
archive.description: Full transition to a compiled based UI, Svelte
Transition to a much darker theme, and migrated CSS to SASS.

archive.title: V3
archive.description: Full rewrite in Preact due to Svelete builds acting up and number of merges. New theme, and layout, with a focus on simplicity and readability.

archive.title: V3.1
archive.description: Added a glow effect and proper background for better readability.
